chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR C. P. No. D – 1065 of 2019 a/w C. Ps. No. D – 369 & 1816 of 2019, 783 of 2020, 239 of 2021, 535 of 2022 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge For directions 1. For orders on CMA No.3720/2023 (Ex./A) 2. For orders on CMA No.3719/2023 (C/A) 3. For hearing of CMA No.810/2023 (C/A) 30.08.2023 Mr. Achar Khan Gabol along with Mr. Israr Ahmed Shah holding brief for Mr. Amir Ali Bhutto, Advocates for the petitioners. Mr. Samiullah Soomro, Advocate for respondents-Workers Welfare Board Sindh. Mr. Zulfiqar Ali Naich, Assistant Advocate General Sindh..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.- On 28.09.2022, all these petitions were disposed of in the terms whereby Selection Committee was directed to consider the case of petitioners, 46 in number, for regularization of their service on merit basis. It appears that Selection Committee interviewed and examined the credentials of all the petitioners and found twenty six, out of them, eligible for regularization. However, meanwhile, listed applications for contempt were filed. In response, a statement has been filed along with recommendations of Selection Committee enumerating names of twenty six candidates/petitioners in tabular form. Learned Counsel for respondents-Workers Welfare Board, who is present today, undertakes that within one month, in the light of recommendations of Selection Committee, service of all the twenty six candidates/petitioners will be regularized and such orders would be issued to them. C. P. No. D – 1065 of 2019 & others Page 2 of 2 Learned Counsel for petitioners are satisfied and we are also satisfied that material compliance has been made; hence, listed contempt applications are disposed of.

No. D – 1065 of 2019 & others Page 2 of 2 Learned Counsel for petitioners are satisfied and we are also satisfied that material compliance has been made; hence, listed contempt applications are disposed of. However, the petitioners, who were not found eligible by Selection Committee, may avail their remedy in accordance with law. Office is directed to place a signed copy of this order in the captioned connected matters. J U D G E J U D G E Abdul Basit
